title: Check if Two Graphs are Isomorphic
description: Basic algorithm to check isomorphism between two graphs with same vertices and edges
code:
// Simple isomorphism check (naive approach)
FUNCTION IsIsomorphic(G1, G2):
 IF |V(G1)| != |V(G2)| OR |E(G1)| != |E(G2)|:
 RETURN false
 
 // Check degree sequences match
 degSeq1 = Sort([degree(v) for v in V(G1)])
 degSeq2 = Sort([degree(v) for v in V(G2)])
 
 IF degSeq1 != degSeq2:
 RETURN false
 
 // For small graphs, try all permutations
 FOR each permutation P of V(G2):
 IF IsAdjacencyPreserving(G1, G2, P):
 RETURN true
 
 RETURN false

FUNCTION IsAdjacencyPreserving(G1, G2, P):
 FOR each edge (u,v) in E(G1):
 IF edge (P[u], P[v]) not in E(G2):
 RETURN false
 RETURN true
explanation: This algorithm checks necessary conditions first (vertex count, edge count, degree sequence), then attempts to find a bijection that preserves adjacency. The naive approach has O(n! × m) complexity, making it impractical for large graphs.

title: Find All Paths Between Two Vertices
description: Algorithm to find all simple paths between two vertices using backtracking
code:
// Find all paths from source to destination
FUNCTION FindAllPaths(G, source, dest):
 paths = []
 currentPath = [source]
 visited = Set()
 DFS(source, dest, currentPath, visited, paths)
 RETURN paths

FUNCTION DFS(current, dest, path, visited, paths):
 IF current == dest:
 paths.add(Copy(path))
 RETURN
 
 FOR each neighbor v of current:
 IF v not in visited:
 visited.add(v)
 path.append(v)
 DFS(v, dest, path, visited, paths)
 path.removeLast()
 visited.remove(v)

// Example usage:
// G with vertices A,B,C,D,E
// FindAllPaths(G, 'A', 'D') returns all paths from A to D
explanation: This backtracking algorithm explores all possible paths from source to destination, avoiding cycles by tracking visited vertices. Each path found is a simple path (no vertex repeats).

title: Check for Cycle Using DFS
description: Algorithm to detect if a cycle exists in an undirected graph
code:
// Detect cycle in undirected graph
FUNCTION HasCycle(G):
 visited = Set()
 parent = Dictionary()
 
 FOR each vertex v in V(G):
 IF v not in visited:
 IF DFSHasCycle(G, v, visited, parent):
 RETURN true
 RETURN false

FUNCTION DFSHasCycle(G, u, visited, parent):
 visited.add(u)
 
 FOR each neighbor v of u:
 IF v not in visited:
 parent[v] = u
 IF DFSHasCycle(G, v, visited, parent):
 RETURN true
 ELSE IF v != parent.get(u):
 // Found a back edge - cycle exists
 RETURN true
 
 RETURN false

// Example: In graph with triangle, DFS from A finds B and C,
// when exploring from B, finds C which is visited and not parent -> cycle
explanation: The algorithm uses DFS with parent tracking. A cycle exists if we find a visited vertex that is not the parent of current vertex. This works for undirected graphs.

title: Generate All Subgraphs of a Graph
description: Algorithm to generate all possible subgraphs of a given graph
explanation: This generates all induced subgraphs by iterating through all vertex subsets. For spanning subgraphs, fix all vertices and iterate through edge subsets. Total induced subgraphs = 2^n - 1 (excluding empty).
